{"key_event":"Suez-era polarization and disputed 1957 elections","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened through labor, student, party, sectarian, Palestinian, and bourgeois organization, with the 1952 strike and Suez-era Arab nationalist mobilization showing real capacity to constrain rulers. State power slipped from the independence peak: Chehabist army professionalization and U.S.-backed presidential authority helped, but refugee pressures, confessional clientelism, fiscal/regulatory limits, and collapsing cross-sectarian legitimacy left central capacity only moderate.","society_change":0.7,"society_power":6.9,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":6.2}
